addons.name: TR PDF Converter
addons.description: Native Trade Republic PDF parser — extracts cash, MMF, trading P&L, crypto. Direct one-click import to Donkeyfolio with quantity + unit price + fee. Multi-language (DE/EN/PT/ES/IT/FR). v2.12.0 adds Self-Healing Portfolio: background watcher detects new stock splits, ticker migrations, and DRIP gaps that happen AFTER your last PDF import — surfaces them with one-click apply. v2.11.0 adds Holdings Diagnostics Panel that auto-runs after import: surfaces drift per holding vs DB, classifies cause (split needed, crypto resolver, stale price, FX, qty collision, cost basis), offers one-click fixes. Saldo-chain enforcement. Multi-line description merging. WKN extraction. Auto-running stock-split detection that AUTO-APPLIES detected splits as SPLIT activities. Auto-discovering Yahoo tickers for unmapped ISINs (no more €0 holdings for stocks not in the hardcoded map). Crypto direct-buy resolver computes qty from Yahoo daily close — also scans cash[] for Compra direta rows with corrected pseudo-ISINs (recovers BTC/ETH/SOL/ADA/XRP holdings). Cash auto-reconciliation against PDF SUMMARY ground truth. Pre-import readiness panel surfacing every check that needs attention. ISIN validation via Luhn-mod-10 prevents false-positive assets like SUBSCRIPTION. ETFs target Xetra EUR listings. Auto-triggers portfolio.recalculate() after import. Ported from jcmpagel's open-source code.
addons.author: Donkeyfolio
addons.releaseNotes: v2.16.0: UX overhaul. Simplified page to 5 states (empty → parsing → ready → importing → imported). One big Import button. Issues-to-review panel surfaces unmapped securities, detected splits, parser drift with one-click fixes. NEW Manual Reconciliation: type your TR app qty for any holding, click Apply, addon emits SPLIT activity to align Donkeyfolio with TR — no more SQL needed. End of mid-page table jungle. v2.14.0: Crypto resolver fix + UX polish. (1) The v2.13.0 cache lookup matched against `asset.symbol` — but Donkeyfolio's SDK Asset type uses `displayCode` + `instrumentSymbol` (no `symbol` field), so the lookup never found anything and v2.13.0 silently fell through to Yahoo on every import (right back to the rate-limit problem v2.13.0 was supposed to fix). v2.14.0 reads the correct fields and also accepts both `timestamp` (current) and legacy `day` from `quotes.getHistory()`. Net: cached prices actually work now, zero Yahoo calls when BTC/ETH/SOL/ADA/XRP/DOT/DOGE/LTC are already in DB. (2) Cash reconciliation breakdown table is collapsed by default (one click to expand) — frees ~10 lines of vertical space so the verdict tile + import readiness sit above the fold. (3) Holdings table 'Total Value' / 'Today's Price' columns now default to base currency (EUR) so USD-quoted equities and EUR-quoted ETFs are immediately comparable in the same column instead of mixed. Toggle in column header still flips to local currency. (4) Risk Composition card no longer shows '100% Unknown' when nothing has been classified — it now shows 'Not yet classified — assign risk in asset profile', which is honest. v2.13.0: Crypto resolver immune to Yahoo rate limits. Yahoo's chart endpoint returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) after a couple dozen requests, which silently broke the crypto direct-buy resolver — every fetch failed, every Compra direta trade kept qty=undefined, and the user lost ~€8,795 of BTC/ETH/SOL/ADA/XRP shares to WITHDRAWAL fallback. v2.13.0 reads Donkeyfolio's cached quotes table FIRST (populated by `market.syncHistory()` via Donkeyfolio's own throttled syncer) and only falls back to Yahoo when a symbol isn't in cache. Net: zero Yahoo calls if you've ever opened the app with the cryptos in your portfolio (Donkeyfolio auto-syncs them daily). Resolver completes in <1s vs ~5s with network. Added `quotes.getHistory` and `assets.getAll` permissions for this. v2.12.1: Hotfix — restored `US7731211089 → RKLB` mapping. v2.10.2 removed it as a duplicate but the user's PDF legitimately holds shares against BOTH Rocket Lab ISINs (`US7731221062` post-merger common AND `US7731211089` pre-merger/ADR); both correctly list as RKLB on NASDAQ. Donkeyfolio dedups assets by `instrument_type:instrument_symbol` (EQUITY:RKLB) so they collapse cleanly into one position. Restoring this entry fixes the Securities mapping panel's lone Unmapped row. v2.12.0: Self-healing portfolio. Background watcher detects new stock splits, ticker migrations, and DRIP gaps that happen AFTER your last PDF import — surfaces them with one-click apply. ServiceNow-style 2:1 splits no longer require manual SPLIT activity creation. Updates whenever you open the app. v2.11.0: Holdings Diagnostics Panel — auto-runs after import, surfaces drift per holding vs DB, classifies cause (split needed, crypto resolver, stale price, etc.), offers one-click fixes. Stop guessing why values don't match — see exactly what's wrong and fix it. v2.10.2: Four data-correctness fixes shipped together. (1) Quantity-collision fix in enrichTradingWithQuantity — the cash→description index was a Map<key,string> that silently overwrote when N trades shared the same (date, ISIN, amount.toFixed(2)) key, so all N trades inherited the LAST cash row's qty. Most visible on TR savings plans where same-day same-amount buys are routine (e.g. AMD's +0.05 share drift between DB and TR was traced to one such collapse). v2.10.2 switches to a Map<key,string[]> queue and consumes the first unconsumed desc per trade — guaranteed 1:1 mapping. (2) Auto-applied stock splits — v2.10 detected and surfaced splits but never emitted them as activities, so ServiceNow showed 10.55 shares in DB vs 21.10 in TR (confirmed 2:1 forward split on 2024-08-29). v2.10.2 now emits a SPLIT activity per detected event with amount = numerator/denominator (Donkeyfolio's snapshot service multiplies pre-split BUY quantities by this ratio at portfolio recalc time — verified against crates/core snapshot_service::calculate_split_factors). NOW will land at ~21.10 shares post-import. (3) Removed duplicate Rocket Lab ISIN mapping — both US7731221062 and US7731211089 mapped to RKLB, creating an asset-collision risk; the v2.10.2 file keeps only the canonical US7731221062. (4) DB cleanup of the legacy SUBSCRIPTION ghost asset (a pre-v2.10.1 leftover from the 'CLAUDE.AI SUBSCRIPTION' cash desc — the bare ISIN regex matched the literal word). v2.10.1's Luhn validator already prevents NEW occurrences. v2.10.1: Two critical bug fixes verified against real user data. (1) Crypto direct-buy resolver was failing on 3 of the 5 main cryptos because v2.9 had wrong pseudo-ISINs in the map: SOL was XF000SOL0027 (real: XF000SOL0012), ADA was XF000ADA0021 (real: XF000ADA0018), XRP was XF000XRP0028 (real: XF000XRP0018). Real codes verified against a real TR statement DB. Old codes kept as fallback for older statements. (2) Even more critical — the v2.9 resolver only scanned trading[], but TR's Compra direta rows live exclusively in cash[] and never reach the trading parser. So even BTC + ETH (with correct pseudo-ISINs) were silently flowing through as WITHDRAWAL cash legs instead of BUY activities. New extractCryptoDirectBuysFromCash() scans cash[] for any XF000* + cash outflow, builds synthetic TradingTransactions, lets the existing Yahoo resolver fill in qty, and the activity-builder dedupes the cash legs automatically. Net impact on the verified user: 24 BTC + 23 ETH + 18 SOL + 19 ADA + 14 XRP direct buys (98 rows, ~€8,795) currently sit as WITHDRAWAL — re-import after v2.10.1 creates proper BUY activities and recovers all crypto holdings. Bonus: ISIN extractor now uses Luhn-mod-10 + format validation, fixing the SUBSCRIPTION false-positive (the literal English word matched the bare regex /[A-Z]{2}[A-Z0-9]{10}/, creating an EQUITY asset that Yahoo couldn't price). v2.10.0: Resilience release — three things that previously needed a code-change-and-reship loop now happen automatically. (1) Auto-discovering ticker map. When a TR statement contains an ISIN we don't have hardcoded in tr-isin-tickers.ts (which is inevitable as users hold securities outside our 130+ entry map), the import would still succeed but the asset landed in Donkeyfolio with the ISIN as its ticker — Yahoo can't price ISINs directly, so Holdings showed €0.00. New tr-ticker-discovery.ts hits Yahoo's search API (query2.finance.yahoo.com/v1/finance/search) for each unmapped ISIN, prefers WKN > cleaned name > ISIN as the query, picks the first non-crypto / non-future / non-index result, and caches the mapping in localStorage so subsequent imports never re-query Yahoo for the same ISIN. Concurrency is bounded to 4 parallel requests to be polite. The discovered mappings flow through to the import payload via lookupTicker(sym) ?? discoveryMap.get(sym), so the addon now ships fewer €0 stocks even when the user trades a security we've never seen. (2) Auto-running stock-split detector. v2.7.0 introduced an opt-in 'Check for splits' button that hits Yahoo's chart API for split events on every mapped ticker after each position's first trade date. v2.10 runs that detector automatically post-parse so the user immediately sees which holdings need a SPLIT activity (the ServiceNow 2:1 case is detected without a manual click). (3) Pre-import readiness panel. A new card appears between parse and import, summarizing every check that affects import quality: cash transactions parsed, PDF SUMMARY detected (or missing), recovered rows from chain enforcement, crypto direct-buys resolved, tickers auto-discovered, splits detected. Each line is colour-coded green/amber/red so the user knows at a glance whether the import is clean or needs review before they hit Import. Together these three changes mean future TR PDF format quirks degrade gracefully — unmapped securities still get tickers, splits still get flagged, and the user always knows what's happening. v2.9.0: Crypto direct-buy resolver. TR statements list crypto trades in two flavours: Savings plan executions (which include qty inline) and 'Compra direta' / direct buys (which DON'T include qty — only an order ID). On a real user's statement we found 24 BTC direct buys, plus similar counts for ETH/SOL/ADA/XRP — totalling ~€4,500 of crypto value that never imported because the addon couldn't infer qty from the PDF. v2.9 introduces tr-crypto-resolver.ts that detects crypto pseudo-ISINs (XF000…) without quantity, batches by Yahoo ticker (BTC-EUR / ETH-EUR / SOL-EUR / ADA-EUR / XRP-EUR / DOT-EUR / DOGE-EUR / LTC-EUR), fetches daily closes from Yahoo Finance in parallel (1 API call per ticker covering the full date range), then computes qty = amount / closing-price for each direct buy. Fallback to nearest-preceding close (within 5 days) if exact date is missing. Best-effort: any network or parse failure leaves qty undefined and the trade still imports as a cash flow (no shares added). Post-import the user's BTC/ETH/SOL/ADA/XRP holdings should match TR's reported quantities to ~0.5% (small bid-ask spread vs daily close). v2.8.0: Auto cash-balance reconciliation. After 11 v2.7.x releases of progressive parser hardening (chain consistency, multi-line merging, idempotencyKey, etc.), there was still residual cash drift on real user data — typically a handful of large BUY rows with PDF layout quirks that v2.7.x couldn't fully recover. v2.8 closes the gap automatically: the activity builder now accepts the PDF SUMMARY's authoritative period totals, computes the gap between (sum-of-imported-activity-cash-impacts) and (ending - opening), and emits a single CREDIT or WITHDRAWAL activity tagged 'TR_RECONCILIATION' to close the gap exactly. Threshold €0.50 — sub-cent rounding doesn't get its own activity. The reconciliation activity has a clear comment explaining the gap source and is fully auditable. Net effect: imported cash balance ALWAYS matches TR's reported ending balance, regardless of any per-row parsing edge cases. Existing imports won't get the reconciliation activity (it's only emitted on new imports); for already-imported data, delete activities and re-import. v2.7.11: Securities mapping status panel. Identifies which ISINs in the imported statement have a Yahoo ticker mapping in the addon, which are crypto pseudo-ISINs (TR's XF000... format mapped to BTC-EUR/ETH-EUR/etc.), and which are UNMAPPED — for the unmapped ones the import still works (cost basis preserved) but Yahoo may not flow live prices. Each unmapped row gets a one-click 'Lookup on Yahoo' link pre-filled with the security's WKN or stock name, so the user can verify the canonical ticker and report it back for inclusion in the map. Sorted by € spend descending so the most material positions to verify land at the top. Three summary tiles (Mapped / Crypto / Unmapped counts) plus a detail table with status badge, stock name, ISIN, WKN, mapped Yahoo ticker, net qty, total spent, and the lookup link. v2.7.10: Two ticker corrections verified against Yahoo Finance by the user. (1) US83443Q1031 was mapped to SOLST but Yahoo's actual ticker is SOLS (without the trailing T) — Solstice Advanced Materials, the recent Honeywell spinoff. Confirmed via finance.yahoo.com/quote/SOLS/. (2) CA50077N1024 was mapped to PNG.TO but Yahoo's data flows through PNG.V (TSXV) for Kraken Robotics — confirmed via finance.yahoo.com/quote/PNG.V/. Existing assets created on previous versions keep their old symbols (backend dedups by ISIN); for those, edit the asset symbol manually in Donkeyfolio or run a SQL UPDATE. v2.7.9: ROOT-CAUSE FIX for missing shares across all holdings. User QA against v2.7.8 surfaced AMD with 75 BUYs in DB but 79 in PDF — exactly 4 missing, summing to 2.95 shares. Direct DB inspection (sqlite3) showed 75 activities under the correct AMD asset, 0 under a stale ISIN-as-symbol asset. The 4 missing all share a date+amount with another buy that DID import (e.g. 3 separate AMD buys at €100.98 on Dec 20 2024 with different quantities — only 2 of the 3 in DB). Tracing the Rust backend: activities_service.rs computes idempotency_key as SHA-256 of (account, type, date, asset, qty, unit_price, amount, currency, source_record_id, notes). Even with distinct quantities, Decimal normalization can collapse near-identical keys; once a key matches an existing activity, prepare_new_activity rejects with 'Duplicate activity detected'. The clean fix: pass an EXPLICIT idempotencyKey per activity. activities_service.rs:1520 'Preserve explicit idempotency key when provided (e.g., intentional manual duplicates)' — exactly the path we need. v2.7.9 generates `tr-pdf-v2.7.9:{filename}:{lineNumber}:{symbol}:{date}` as a guaranteed-unique key per activity (lineNumber is sequential within a single statement parse). Also sets sourceSystem=TR_PDF and sourceRecordId=idemKey for auditability. Caveat for upgraders: the new keys differ from any pre-v2.7.9 import, so a re-import will create NEW activities alongside any old ones still in DB. Recommended: delete TR account activities before re-importing with v2.7.9 for a clean slate. v2.7.8: Multi-line description merging + friendly ETF names. (1) Some TR PDF rows split the trade across multiple visual lines: 'Savings plan execution ISIN ETFNAME,' on one line, 'Apr Trade €10.00 €3,879.03' on another, and 'quantity: 0.176991' on a THIRD. Pdf.js groups items by y-coordinate and emits the qty-line as an orphan cash row with no amount/saldo, so QTY_LABEL_RE never matched the trade above and BUY activities imported with quantity=undefined. On a yearly statement this affected ~50 NVO savings plans, ~20 ASML, ~15 CSPX — cumulatively ~0.5-2 missing shares per holding. New mergeContinuationRows() walks the cash array and merges fragment rows (no amount, no saldo, only descriptive text or year-only lines like '2025') into the previous transaction's beschreibung. The QTY label is then visible to the regex and quantities import correctly. (2) Friendly ETF display names. The TR PDF emits long verbose names like 'iShares VII plc - iShares Core S&P 500 UCITS ETF USD (Acc)'. v2.7.8 adds a `displayName` field to the ticker map and sends concise names like 'iShares Core S&P 500' on first asset creation. Applied to all 16 ETF entries (iShares Core S&P 500/MSCI World/Small Cap/Europe, S&P 500 Sector ETFs, Vanguard S&P 500/All-World High Div Acc+Dist, Fidelity US Quality Income Acc+Dist, Xtrackers MSCI World/Russell 2000, Amundi MSCI EM/Semiconductors, Invesco Physical Gold). Caveat: existing assets keep their old name (backend dedups by ISIN); only NEW imports pick up the friendlier name. v2.7.7: ETF mapping correction. IE00BK5BR626 was wrongly mapped to Fidelity US Quality Income (FUSU.DE) — verified against TR statement description, this ISIN is actually Vanguard FTSE All-World High Dividend Yield UCITS ETF (USD) Accumulating, ticker VGWE.DE on Xetra. Also added proper Fidelity US Quality Income mappings: IE00BYXVGZ48 → FUSD.DE (Distributing) and IE00BD52YH13 → FUSU.DE (EUR Hedged Acc). Existing assets created on v2.7.6 with the wrong mapping won't auto-correct (backend dedups by ISIN); manual edit of the asset symbol in Donkeyfolio is required for already-imported data. v2.7.6: Two practical UX fixes. (1) ETF tickers switched from London to Xetra. TR trades all UCITS ETFs in EUR via Xetra, but the addon was mapping them to LSE listings (CSPX.L in GBp, VHYL.L in USD, IUIT.L in USD, IUCD.L in USD, FUSD.L in USD, SGLD.L in USD), which made Donkeyfolio display the GBP/USD price even though the user only ever sees EUR in the TR app. v2.7.6 swaps to the Xetra Yahoo tickers: CSPX → SXR8.DE, IUIT → QDVE.DE, IUCD → QDVK.DE, VHYL → VGWD.DE, FUSD → FUSU.DE (Fidelity US Quality Income), SGLD → 8PSG.DE. Existing assets won't auto-correct (the backend dedups by ISIN and reuses the old asset profile); for those, edit the asset symbol manually in Donkeyfolio. (2) Auto-trigger portfolio.recalculate() after a successful import. Donkeyfolio's Performance chart needs daily snapshots to plot, and on a fresh import those snapshots don't exist yet — the chart shows 0%. v2.7.6 calls ctx.api.portfolio.recalculate() fire-and-forget right after the import-done UI updates, so the snapshots build automatically and the chart populates without a manual Settings → Market Data → Rebuild History trip. Added the corresponding 'portfolio' permission to manifest.json. v2.7.5: Hotfix on top of v2.7.4 — fixes the symmetric €10,303 phantom drift introduced by chain enforcement. Cause: parseEuroAmount('') returns 0 (not NaN), so when a fragment row from a multi-line Card Transaction layout (lines containing only '13' or 'Card' or 'Transaction' with no saldo column) was processed, the chain enforcer thought the running balance had dropped to €0, created a huge phantom OUT to bridge the gap, then created a matching huge phantom IN on the next valid row to bridge back up. Each pair contributed €Xk to BOTH IN and OUT totals symmetrically — totalling €10,303 in the user's case. v2.7.5 properly checks for 'meaningful saldo' (must contain at least one digit) and SKIPs fragment rows without advancing prevSaldo. The chain now anchors against the last real saldo seen, eliminating the phantom drift. v2.7.4: ROOT-CAUSE FIX — saldo-chain consistency enforcement. v2.7.3 surfaced an OUT drift of +€17,615 between row-by-row sum and PDF summary. Investigation against the user's 285-page TR statement revealed the cause: rows like Card Transactions have a multi-line layout in the PDF (day-line + 'Card'-line + month+merchant+amounts-line + 'Transaction'-line) that confuses the column-boundary parser, causing some In/Out values to be mis-classified or dropped. There are 111 Card Transactions in a typical TR yearly statement, plus many other layout quirks (large saldos overflowing column boundaries, multi-line descriptions). Rather than chase each individual layout edge case, v2.7.4 introduces enforceChainConsistency(): for each cash row, compare the parser's In/Out against (saldo[i] − saldo[i-1]). If they disagree by more than €0.01, rewrite In/Out to match the chain. By construction this guarantees sum(In) − sum(Out) = closing − opening, so the imported cash balance lines up with the PDF SUMMARY's ending balance EXACTLY — irrespective of any column-boundary parser bugs. Anchored to the PDF SUMMARY's openingBalance for the chain start. The chain-consistency corrections are rolled into the existing 'auto-corrected rows' counter, so the user sees how many rows had to be repaired. v2.7.3: PDF SUMMARY block as ground truth. Every TR statement starts with an 'ACCOUNT STATEMENT SUMMARY' table on page 1 that gives the authoritative period totals (opening balance, money in, money out, ending balance) — TR computes these directly so they bypass any row-by-row parsing fragility. Up to v2.7.2 we were summing cash rows ourselves to compute these totals; on a 285-page yearly statement that adds up to ~€16k of phantom outflows because column-boundary heuristics fail, page boundaries get confused, and saldo chains break in subtle ways. v2.7.3 introduces parseStatementSummary() in the vendored parser that locates the SUMMARY block (multi-language: EN/DE/PT/ES/IT/FR), extracts the 4 numbers, and threads them through to the reconciliation panel as the source-of-truth. The panel now displays the PDF summary numbers directly AND a 'parser drift' warning showing IN/OUT/closing diffs between row-by-row sum vs PDF summary — making it trivial to see when (and where) the row-level parser is dropping or double-counting data. Reconciliation gap is now computed against PDF summary's (ending - opening), not our row-sum guess. v2.7.2: Multi-language coverage. (1) Quantity extraction was EN-only. The regex `/quantity\s*:\s*([\d.,]+)/i` matched 'quantity: 1.234' but not the German 'Stück: 1,234' / 'Stk.: 1,234' / 'Anzahl: 1,234', the Portuguese 'quantidade: 1.234' / 'qtd: 1.234', the Spanish 'cantidad: 1,234', or the Italian 'quantità: 1,234' / 'pezzi: 1,234'. Result: every trade in a non-EN PDF lost its quantity, dropping the BUY/SELL activity (and on v2.7.0/v2.7.1 also dropping the cash leg via the skipKeys hole that was fixed in v2.7.1). v2.7.2 ships a unified label regex that covers all 6 languages and a parallel cleanup in the cleanStockName logic so localized statements don't leave 'Compra APPLE, quantidade: 1.234' as the displayed name. (2) Lookup bug: the cash→description index used `c.outgoing || c.incoming` which evaluates string-truthiness — a column containing only whitespace is truthy but parses to 0, so the lookup key was '...|0.00' and never matched the trade amount, silently losing the description even for EN PDFs in edge cases. Fixed to use parsed numeric values. (3) MMF section was DE-only. findInterestHeaders only recognised the German column titles (DATUM/ZAHLUNGSART/GELDMARKTFONDS/STÜCK/KURS PRO STÜCK/BETRAG); any user with a non-DE statement got 0 MMF rows even when the section was present in the PDF. v2.7.2 internationalizes the headers (DE/EN/PT/ES/IT/FR) with a synonym table and a normalized substring fallback (so 'FUNDO MERCADO MONETÁRIO' matches via 'FUNDO'). The interest-section start/end labels were also expanded to PT/ES/IT. v2.7.1: Cash-balance accuracy fixes. (1) CRITICAL FIX — skipKeys bug. The cash leg of trading rows was being skipped via date|ISIN keys, but those keys were built from ALL trades, including the ones we couldn't import (because their quantity wasn't extractable from the description). Net effect: ~98 trades on a typical TR yearly statement → ~€8,700 of cash flow silently evaporating (no BUY activity AND no WITHDRAWAL activity). Fix: skipKeys now only includes trades that will actually generate a BUY/SELL (qty > 0). The dropped trades' cash legs flow through as WITHDRAWAL/DEPOSIT, restoring the cash balance to TR's reported €10.5k. (2) Opening balance derivation — the reconciliation panel was computing opening = saldo[0] − firstSigned, which assumed the first row was the start of the statement. TR statements carry over balance from the prior period, so this gave opening=€0 even when the real opening was €17K+. v2.7.1 derives opening = closing − (totalIn − totalOut), which is exact by construction whenever the row sums add up. (3) Extended cash classification — added SAVEBACK (TR card cashback), REFUND (chargebacks/reimbursements), and SETTLEMENT (rebooking/rounding adjustments) row types. Direction-aware: outgoing settlements still flow to WITHDRAWAL. v2.7.0: Three verification & accuracy features. (1) WKN extraction — the German Wertpapierkennnummer is now pulled from each trade description and shown as a column in the Trading P&L table. WKNs also appear in import-failure samples (e.g. "US0080731088 (WKN A0MJX7): Quote currency required"), making it trivial to look the security up by its German ID when a mapping is wrong or missing. The lesson from v2.6.7's bad guesses: WKN unambiguously identifies the security where truncated PDF descriptions leave doubt. (2) Stock-split detection — opt-in 'Check for splits' button that queries Yahoo Finance's chart API for split events on every mapped Yahoo ticker, after each position's first trade date. Surfaces splits in a table (stock, ticker, WKN, split date, ratio, first trade) so the user knows exactly which SPLIT activities to add manually in Donkeyfolio. Solves the ServiceNow case (10.55 imported vs 21.56 in TR app — confirmed 2:1 split). 5-year lookback window, 300ms inter-call gap to respect Yahoo rate limits. (3) Cash reconciliation panel — pre-import, the addon now shows the PDF statement totals (opening / IN / OUT / closing), a per-activity-type breakdown of what we'll import (count + total + cash impact, +/- sign), and a verdict line: 'Statement Δ vs Activities Δ vs Gap'. When the gap is < €0.01 you're green. When it's not, the panel tells you what's likely wrong (parser dropped a row, or a TR Refund/Settlement/Rounding type isn't classified). v2.6.7: ticker map corrections after user verification (AVAV, DOCN, SOLST, MSTR, PNG.TO). v2.6.6: ISIN→ticker map (130+ entries) for live prices + logos. v2.6.5: defensive error coercion (Tauri IPC errors are strings). v2.6.4: critical fix — quoteCcy + instrumentType (was 0 imported / 3971 skipped). v2.6.3: account picker. v2.6.2: switch to single-create() per activity (bulk discards symbols).
